changes.summary: Simplified nav event listeners and player asset loading for cleaner, more reliable behavior across the app.
"Manage keepers" link on the AFL Fantasy homepage now goes to the Keeper Planner on afl-fantasy.com instead of a broken internal page.
Removed the stray gap above the AFL Fantasy homepage — the hero and Schefter Report now sit flush at the top of their columns instead of floating below an empty band.
Preseason homepage hero promos (tag window, team purchase deadline, release deadline) now use a darker, readable blue so the white pill label and countdown stand out instead of washing out.
AFL Fantasy's Owner Activity report now tracks waiver claims instead of auction wins — AFL is a keeper league with no auction, so the old "Auctions" column never reflected real activity.

Cutting a player now works even when your offseason roster is over the in-season limit. We switched the cut to the same add/drop path MFL's own site uses for owners, which allows trimming an over-limit roster — so any owner can release a player during the cutdown. Cutting a player that was already dropped now shows a clear 'refresh your roster' message instead of a confusing permissions error. Also fixed AFL confirmation copy that wrongly referenced an "auction pool" (AFL drafts, it doesn't auction).

AFL Fantasy conferences now carry their own American League (red) and National League (blue) logos across the conference standings headers, the division headers, and the homepage playoff and draft previews, and the Premier League / D-League marks now appear on the homepage tier standings preview too.

The Division / Playoff / All-Play tabs on the standings page now actually switch views (they were dead before), and the homepage "Full bracket" and "Full standings" links no longer land on a blank page.

AFL Fantasy got its own identity: the top "Back to MFL" bar and footer now use a deep navy, the header nav icons are navy (turning red on hover), and homepage accents — section borders, icons, and links — are now AFL red instead of the borrowed blue. TheLeague's look is unchanged.

The Final / Base / History tabs on the Draft Order page now actually switch views — they were silently dead when you arrived via in-app navigation. An unrecognized view in the URL now falls back to the Final order instead of rendering a blank page.
